title: Redundant nullability annotations should be removed
description: Nullability annotations such as @Nullable or @NotNull are used to indicate whether a variable, parameter, or return value can be null. However, redundant nullability annotations can clutter the code and reduce readability. This rule detects and flags unnecessary nullability annotations that do not add value, helping to keep the codebase clean and maintainable.
recommendation: Remove any redundant nullability annotations from your code. Only use nullability annotations where they provide meaningful information about the contract of a method, parameter, or field.
impact: Redundant annotations can mislead developers and make code harder to read and maintain.

checks.message: Remove any redundant nullability annotations from your code. Only use nullability annotations where they provide meaningful information about the contract of a method, parameter, or field.
